roadmap.goal: Ship OAuth-authenticated work-tracking connections (Jira 3LO + Azure DevOps Entra ID OAuth) as the canonical Premium-licensed alternative to PAT/API-token authentication, plus an admin-visible OAuth Health tile surfacing setup-success and refresh-success KPIs. Source: ADO Epic #2438 with 6 child stories (#4967–4972) plus follow-on Story #5018 (popup reconnect). The implementation extends the established AuthenticationMethodSchema + WorkTrackingSystemConnectionOption + ICryptoService surfaces (per DESIGN reuse analysis) and introduces a single new entity (OAuthCredential), a single new outbound port (IOAuthProvider), and a single new controller (OAuthController). 4 slices of crafter dispatch: Slice 01 Jira OAuth foundation, Slice 02 refresh + Health tile, Slice 03 ADO OAuth, Slice 04 popup-mediated reconnect (Story #5018, ADR-011). Plus a release-readiness phase wiring the oauth-smoke JOB into ci_e2e.yml.
roadmap.overview: Six phases. Phase 01 (Slice 01) is the foundation: it lands the new entity, the IOAuthProvider abstraction with its first concrete user (JiraOAuthProvider), the controller, the auth-strategy refactor (DDD-9), the premium gate (DDD-6), the HMAC state token (DDD-8), and the Jira-side UI. Phase 01 step 14 is the walking-skeleton moment — Playwright scenario 1 goes green end-to-end against the StubOAuthProvider. Phase 02 adds silent token refresh (DDD-7 single-flight via ConcurrentDictionary<int, SemaphoreSlim>) plus the OAuth Health tile folded in per OQ-DV1. Phase 03 ships AdoOAuthProvider — by design, the diff touches only the new provider + DI + auth-method schema entry + docs (Slice 03 AC #5 invariant). Phase 05 wires the release-gated oauth-smoke JOB into the existing ci_e2e.yml (workflow consolidation per maintainer principle) + ci-learnings + observability docs. Phase 06 (Slice 04, added 2026-05-16 for Story #5018) migrates the OAuth handshake from a full-page redirect to a popup window mediated by a same-origin landing page (ADR-011 Option A, postMessage). The popup phase consists of: an additive DB-level UNIQUE index on OAuthCredential (DDD-13), a 2-line redirect target change in OAuthController.Callback (DDD-14), a new useOAuthPopup hook + OAuthPopupComplete landing page (DDD-11/12/15/16), three call-site migrations (ReconnectBanner, OAuthAuthForm, CreateConnectionWizard), and one Playwright walking-skeleton-migration scenario. Phase 06 step 06-09 is the slice-04 walking-skeleton moment — the Playwright popup-migration scenario goes green end-to-end. Test placement is symmetric: every Playwright scenario in OAuthConnection.spec.ts has a corresponding production step; every backend integration test (BI-1..BI-4 plus the Story #5018 redirect-target and UNIQUE-index tests) has its own unignore / drive-to-green step in the slice that owns it.

validation.reviewer: self-review against automated quality gate (Phase 1.c) — Eclipse + Architect + Forge + Sentinel already reviewed DISCUSS/DESIGN/DEVOPS/DISTILL in the consolidated final review gate on 2026-05-14
validation.notes:
Self-review against the 6-check automated gate: (1) AC coupling — every criteria references public APIs (HTTP endpoints, public service methods, persisted entity columns, DOM-observable UI); no private-method references. (2) Decomposition ratio — 37 steps across ~50 files-to-modify = 0.74, well under 2.5 gate. (3) Identical patterns — Phase 01 unique-per-step (entity → ports → strategies → service → provider → controller → DI → connector refactor → FE adapter → FE form → integration → backend integration tests → Playwright → docs); Phase 02 unique-per-step; Phase 03 mirrors Phase 01 structure but at half the depth (provider + schema + connector refactor + FE + tests + docs) — intentional parallelism, not duplication, justified by the provider-agnostic abstraction (DDD-3); Phase 04 + Phase 05 are short and distinct. (4) Validation-only — zero steps with empty files_to_modify. (5) Step-ID format — all match ^\d{2}-\d{2}$. (6) DISTILL linkage — every step lists test_file + scenario_name (Playwright .spec.ts + .feature file path, plus the corresponding BI-N integration test for steps that drive those tests). All 12 main+smoke Playwright scenarios mapped (3 to Phase 01, 4 to Phase 02, 2 to Phase 03, 1 to Phase 04, 2 smoke to Phase 05). All 4 backend integration tests (BI-1..BI-4) mapped (BI-1+BI-2 to Phase 01 step 13, BI-3 to Phase 02 step 07, BI-4 to Phase 04 step 03). Walking skeleton scenario 1 (Slice 01 Jira OAuth happy path) goes green at step 01-14, the last green-driving step of Phase 01 before docs. Zero blockers.

Phase 06 self-review (added 2026-05-16 by Morgan, nw-solution-architect) against the same 6-check automated gate: (1) AC coupling — every Phase 06 criteria references public APIs (HTTP redirect targets, DOM-observable UI behaviour, hook return contracts as exported by the new module, persisted entity invariants enforced by DB-level UNIQUE); zero private-method references; the `useOAuthPopup` hook is tested through its sole exported function `openOAuthPopup` and the discriminated-union `OAuthPopupResult` it returns. (2) Decomposition ratio — 9 Phase-06 steps across 14 distinct files-to-modify (incl. the EF migration directories as one logical artifact each) = 1.56 average, well under the 2.5 gate. (3) Identical patterns — Phase 06 steps are unique-per-step (EF migration → controller redirect rewrite → hook → landing page + router → three call-site migrations each with their own concerns → ModifyConnectionSettings wiring + Playwright + optional docs). The three call-site migration steps (06-06 ReconnectBanner, 06-07 OAuthAuthForm, 06-08 CreateConnectionWizard) follow the same `globalThis.location.assign → openOAuthPopup` substitution shape; this is intentional parallelism (same DDD-14 substitution at three sites with different UX wiring per call site), not duplication — the wizard's draft-connection-reuse-on-retry logic (per Iteration-3 Q2-grounded decision) and the inline-alert vs. banner-stays-visible vs. step-stays-mounted differences make each step's UX wiring distinct. Justified explicitly per feature-delta line 1097–1099. (4) Validation-only — zero Phase-06 steps with empty files_to_modify. (5) Step-ID format — 06-01..06-09 all match ^\d{2}-\d{2}$. (6) DISTILL linkage — every Phase-06 step lists test_file + scenario_name traced to a specific RED-state test from the DISTILL commit 07875566. The walking-skeleton-migration Playwright scenario (Slice 04 demo) goes green at step 06-09. The OQ-5018-3 Webkit gold-test remains explicitly deferred (per DISTILL commit's `.skip @deferred` marker preserved at step 06-09); not in scope for this DELIVER pass. Reviewer for Phase 06: self-review against the automated gate; reviewed_at 2026-05-16. Earlier reviewer line + reviewed_at preserved for phases 01–05.

phases.name: Slice 01 — Jira OAuth foundation
phases.description: The foundation slice. Lands every new architectural surface: OAuthCredential entity, IOAuthProvider port + registry, IOAuthStateTokenIssuer (HMAC), IWorkTrackingAuthStrategy refactor (DDD-9) with three concrete strategies (PAT, JiraBasic, OAuthBearer), IOAuthService + OAuthService, JiraOAuthProvider, OAuthController, AuthenticationMethodSchema premium flag + jira.oauth entry, DI wiring in Program.cs, IServiceConfig.BaseUrl, JiraWorkTrackingConnector refactor, WorkTrackingSystemConnectionDto.RequiresReconnect placeholder, and the Jira-side UI (OAuthService.ts, OAuthAuthForm.tsx, AuthMethodDropdown premium-gate logic, connection settings page integration). Walking-skeleton scenario 1 (Playwright) goes green at step 01-14. Backend integration tests BI-1 + BI-2 unignored at step 01-13. Slice 03 (ADO) will then add the second concrete IOAuthProvider with zero changes to anything else — that's the integrity proof.

steps.name: Refactor IOAuthProvider.RefreshTokenAsync to take OAuthRefreshContext (interface correction)
steps.description:
Interleaved correction step inserted 2026-05-14 after step 01-07 surfaced that the locked IOAuthProvider.RefreshTokenAsync(string refreshToken, CancellationToken) signature cannot accommodate real OAuth IdPs (Atlassian, Entra ID) that require client_id + client_secret on the refresh-token endpoint.

RED: extend `JiraOAuthProviderTest.cs` (created in step 01-07) by RESTORING the two assertions the crafter dropped during 01-07 — the form body for grant_type=refresh_token MUST contain client_id and client_secret. Also assert the new signature compiles against the new context type.

GREEN: 1) Create `Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Models/OAuth/OAuthRefreshContext.cs` as a record `(string RefreshToken, string ClientId, string ClientSecret)`. 2) Change `IOAuthProvider.RefreshTokenAsync(string refreshToken, CancellationToken)` to `RefreshTokenAsync(OAuthRefreshContext context, CancellationToken)`. 3) Update `JiraOAuthProvider.RefreshTokenAsync` to use context.RefreshToken/ClientId/ClientSecret in the form body. 4) Update `OAuthBearerAuthStrategy` if it references RefreshTokenAsync directly (likely not — it goes through IOAuthService.EnsureFreshTokenAsync). 5) Update `NotYetImplementedOAuthService` if needed (the stub for IOAuthService, not IOAuthProvider — likely no change). 6) Confirm no production code currently calls IOAuthProvider.RefreshTokenAsync (it ships in step 02-01); the compile-fix is in the test file and JiraOAuthProvider only.

The new context wraps refresh_token + client_id + client_secret. No production callers exist yet (EnsureFreshTokenAsync ships in step 02-01); the change is contained to the interface + JiraOAuthProvider + tests.\n\nStep-ID: 01-08`

steps.name: Persist OAuthStateSecret across restarts via IDataProtectionProvider
steps.description:
Added 2026-05-14 after step 01-02 surfaced that the auto-generated OAuthStateSecret currently lives in-memory only — each boot regenerates it, invalidating any outstanding 15-minute state tokens. Per OQ-D1's design intent (auto-generate AND persist), this step wires ASP.NET Core's built-in `IDataProtectionProvider` (already in the Lighthouse stack for cookie/auth-token protection) to seed and persist the secret.

RED: extend the test from step 01-02 with a new `OAuthStateSecretPersistenceTest.cs` integration test that (a) starts a `WebApplicationFactory<Program>`, (b) reads the in-memory `IServiceConfig.OAuthStateSecret` value, (c) disposes the factory and starts a SECOND factory pointing at the same data-protection key location, (d) asserts the second factory's `OAuthStateSecret` matches the first.

GREEN: refactor `EnsureOAuthStateSecret` in `Program.cs` to use `IDataProtectionProvider`:
  - Resolve `IDataProtectionProvider` from the service container.
  - Create a `IDataProtector` with purpose string `"Lighthouse.OAuth.StateSecret.v1"`.
  - On first boot: generate 32 random bytes, protect them via the data protector, persist the protected blob via the existing config or a tiny new persistent-secrets table (or via `IDataProtectionBuilder.PersistKeysToFileSystem` / `PersistKeysToDbContext` whichever fits — read the existing data-protection setup to match).
  - On subsequent boots: unprotect and reuse.

Cross-boot test must pass. Pre-existing 4 tests in step 01-02 still pass.

COMMIT: `feat(oauth): persist OAuthStateSecret across restarts via IDataProtectionProvider`
steps.scenario_name: OAuthStateSecret survives a process restart (fulfils OQ-D1 design intent)
red_to_green_tests: Two WebApplicationFactory<Program> instances pointing at the same data-protection key location resolve the same OAuthStateSecret value
criteria: OAuthStateSecret persists across process restarts.
Pre-existing OAuthStateTokenIssuerTest cases still green.
No new NuGet dependency (uses built-in Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection).
Failure mode acceptable: if the data-protection key store is wiped, the secret regenerates (current behaviour — not a regression).

deferral_history.reason: Step 01-02 crafter found no existing persistent-secret precedent in the codebase, took the in-memory deferral path under the prompt's 'make the reasonable call' clause, and flagged it transparently in the commit body. PO accepted the deferral (2026-05-14) and added this step to track the persistence fix as release-readiness work — v1 is shippable with in-memory regeneration (15-min token lifetime caps the user impact); GA should not ship without persistence.

phases.name: Slice 04 — OAuth popup reconnect (Story #5018)
phases.description: Added 2026-05-16. Migrates the OAuth handshake from a full-page redirect to a popup window mediated by a same-origin landing page, per ADR-011 (Option A locked: postMessage from /oauth/popup-complete; Option B BroadcastChannel pre-approved as a same-shape fallback if the OQ-5018-3 Webkit gold-test ever fires — the gold-test itself remains deferred to a future slice). The slice is intentionally minimal: 1 EF migration (DDD-13 UNIQUE index), 1 LINQ simplification co-traveller (DDD-13 GroupBy collapse), 1 controller redirect-target rewrite (DDD-14, 2 lines), 1 new frontend hook (DDD-11/12/15), 1 new frontend landing page + router wiring (DDD-14/16), 3 call-site migrations (ReconnectBanner / OAuthAuthForm / CreateConnectionWizard) that swap globalThis.location.assign for openOAuthPopup, 1 ModifyConnectionSettings wiring + Playwright walking-skeleton-migration scenario. Backend deltas are tiny by design; the slice surface is mostly frontend. Test RED scaffolds already exist in the repo from DISTILL commit 07875566 (test(oauth): RED scaffolds + acceptance tests for Story #5018 popup reconnect). The OQ-5018-3 Webkit gold-test (Safari window.opener retention probe) is explicitly DEFERRED — the `.skip @deferred` marker on that Playwright test is preserved in step 06-09, not removed; deferral resolution is a separate future DELIVER follow-up.

steps.description: RED: `Lighthouse.Frontend/src/hooks/useOAuthPopup.ts` is on disk as a scaffold from DISTILL commit 07875566 — the exported function `openOAuthPopup` and the types `OAuthPopupResult` / `OAuthPopupStatus` / `UseOAuthPopup` exist with the correct signatures; the body throws `Not yet implemented — RED scaffold (Story #5018 DISTILL)`. Seven Vitest tests in `useOAuthPopup.test.ts` are RED. GREEN: implement `openOAuthPopup(authorizationUrl: string): Promise<OAuthPopupResult>`. Internals (per feature-delta line 1096 + DDD-15): (a) call `window.open(authorizationUrl, '_blank', 'popup=yes,width=600,height=700,left=...,top=...')` with centred geometry; (b) if the call returns `null`, resolve with `{ status: 'popup_blocked' }` immediately; (c) subscribe to `window.addEventListener('message', handler)` with `handler` filtering by `event.origin === window.location.origin && event.data?.type === 'oauth.complete'` (DDD-12 + DDD-16 — same-origin only, message-type-filtered); (d) start a `setInterval(() => popup.closed, 500)` poller with a 90 s wall-clock guard (DDD-15; per Iteration-2 deferral resolution, name this 90 s constant with a single one-line WHY comment referencing the state-token TTL invariant — this is one of the rare 'WHY' comments CLAUDE.md allows); (e) resolve with `{ status, connectionId?, reason? }` parsed from the first matching `oauth.complete` message; resolve with `{ status: 'cancelled' }` if the popup closes without a message inside the 90 s window; resolve with `{ status: 'error', reason: 'timeout' }` if the 90 s elapses with neither close nor message; (f) always remove the listener and clear the interval in a `finally` block. steps.name: CreateConnectionWizard migration — startOAuthHandshake uses popup; delete ?oauth=success resume branch; reuse draft on retry
steps.description: RED: `CreateConnectionWizard.test.tsx` carries two new cases from DISTILL commit 07875566, both RED: (3a) `advances to step 3 when the OAuth popup resolves with success`, (3b) `renders the popup-blocked alert on step 2 and re-enables Connect when the OAuth popup is blocked`. The old `?oauth=success&connectionId=` resume-URL test (lines 846–887) and its sibling cancel-after-resume test were DELETED in DISTILL per DDD-14 (feature-delta lines 1484–1485). GREEN: in `CreateConnectionWizard.tsx`, modify `startOAuthHandshake` (lines 328–365) to: (a) save the draft `WorkTrackingSystemConnection` if not already saved (first call); (b) call `await openOAuthPopup(authorizationUrl)` instead of `globalThis.location.assign(authorizationUrl)`; (c) on `status === 'success'` advance the wizard to step 3 inline — no remount, no URL-driven resume; (d) on `status === 'popup_blocked'` keep step 2 mounted, preserve form state, render an inline `Alert severity="error"` with the DDD-15 locked copy, and re-enable the Connect button; (e) on `status === 'cancelled'` keep step 2 mounted, render `Alert severity="info"` with `OAuth was cancelled. Click Connect to try again.`, re-enable Connect; (f) on `status === 'error'` keep step 2 mounted, render `Alert severity="error"` surfacing `result.reason`, re-enable Connect; (g) clear the inline alert on the next Connect click before re-invoking `openOAuthPopup`. **Draft connection retry semantics (Iteration-3 Q2-grounded decision, feature-delta line 1099)**: on every retry after a non-success status, **REUSE the existing draft `connectionId` from the first `startOAuthHandshake` call — do NOT re-create the draft**. Rationale: the draft is a hard prerequisite for the `POST /api/oauth/{provider}/connect` controller call (it needs a DB row to resolve provider config and mint the state-token's connectionId claim), not a survive-the-redirect artifact, so it must exist by the time `openOAuthPopup` is called. Moving draft creation into step 3 was considered and rejected during DESIGN. (h) DELETE the `?oauth=success&connectionId=` resume branch from the mount-time `useEffect` (dead per DDD-14). COMMIT: `feat(oauth): migrate CreateConnectionWizard.startOAuthHandshake to popup + delete dead resume-URL branch (DDD-14/15)`.
